question: How many schools with an average score in Math greater than 400 in the SAT test are exclusively virtual?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T2.School) FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.Virtual = 'F' AND T1.AvgScrMath > 400

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM schools AS T1 JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds WHERE T1.Virtual = 'F' AND T2.AvgScrMath > 400

SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM schools AS T1 
JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds 
WHERE T1.Virtual = 'F' AND T2.AvgScrMath > 400

FROM schools 
JOIN satscores ON schools.CDSCode = satscores.cds

question: Please list the lowest three eligible free rates for students aged 5-17 in continuation schools.
gold_sql: SELECT `Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` / `Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` FROM frpm WHERE `Educational Option Type` = 'Continuation School' AND `Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` / `Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` IS NOT NULL ORDER BY `Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` / `Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` ASC LIMIT 3

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT f.CDSCode, 
       f.School Name, 
       f."Percent (%) Eligible Free (Ages 5-17)" AS Rate
FROM frpm f
JOIN schools s ON f.CDSCode = s.CDSCode
WHERE s.`School Type` = 'Continuation'
AND ((f.`Low Grade` <= 5 AND f.`High Grade` >= 17) OR (f.`Low Grade` <= 17 AND f.`High Grade` >= 5))
ORDER BY f."Percent (%) Eligible Free (Ages 5-17)"
LIMIT 3

SELECT 
    frpm.CDSCode, 
    frpm.School Name, 
    frpm.Percent (%) Eligible Free (Ages 5-17) AS EligibleFreeRate
FROM 
    schools
JOIN 
    frpm ON schools.CDSCode = frpm.CDSCode
WHERE 
    frpm.[Enrollment (Ages 5-17)] > 0
ORDER BY 
    frpm.[Percent (%) Eligible Free (Ages 5-17)] ASC
LIMIT 3;

SELECT 
    frpm.School AS SchoolName,
    (frpm.[Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)] / frpm.[Enrollment (Ages 5-17)]) AS EligibleFreeRate
FROM 
    frpm
JOIN 
    schools ON frpm.CDSCode = schools.CDSCode
WHERE 
    schools.EILName = 'Continuation' AND frpm.[Low Grade] = 'K' AND frpm.[High Grade] = '12'
ORDER BY 
    EligibleFreeRate ASC
LIMIT 3

SELECT 
    frpm."Percent (%) Eligible Free (Ages 5-17)" AS FreeRate,
    frpm.School_Name,
    frpm.Enrollment_Ages_5_17
FROM 
    frpm
JOIN 
    schools ON frpm.CDSCode = schools.CDSCode
WHERE 
    schools.School_Type = 'Continuation'
ORDER BY 
    frpm."Percent (%) Eligible Free (Ages 5-17)" ASC
LIMIT 3;

SELECT f."CDSCode", f."Percent (%) Eligible Free (Ages 5-17)" AS "Eligible Free Rate" FROM frpm f JOIN schools s ON f."CDSCode" = s."CDSCode" WHERE s."School Type" = 'Continuation' AND f."Percent (%) Eligible Free (Ages 5-17)" IS NOT NULL AND (s."GSserved" LIKE '%5%' OR s."GSserved" LIKE '%6%' OR s."GSserved" LIKE '%7%') ORDER BY "Eligible Free Rate" ASC LIMIT 3;

SELECT frpm.[Percent (%) Eligible Free (Ages 5-17)]
FROM frpm 
INNER JOIN schools ON frpm.CDSCode = schools.CDSCode 
WHERE schools.SchoolType = 'Continuation' 
ORDER BY frpm.[Percent (%) Eligible Free (Ages 5-17)] ASC 
LIMIT 3;

question: What is the number of SAT test takers of the schools with the highest FRPM count for K-12 students?
gold_sql: SELECT NumTstTakr FROM satscores WHERE cds = ( SELECT CDSCode FROM frpm ORDER BY `FRPM Count (K-12)` DESC LIMIT 1 )

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT SUM(st.NumTstTakr) AS TotalSATTestTakers
FROM (
    SELECT frpm.CDSCode
    FROM frpm
    ORDER BY frpm.`FRPM Count (K-12)` DESC
    LIMIT 1
) AS top_frpm
JOIN satscores st ON top_frpm.CDSCode = st.cds;

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT T3.NumTstTakr 
    FROM frpm AS T1 
    JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode 
    JOIN satscores AS T3 ON T2.CDSCode = T3.cds 
    WHERE T2.School Type = 'K-12 Schools (Public)' 
    AND T1.`FRPM Count (K-12)` > 0 
    ORDER BY T1.`FRPM Count (K-12)` DESC 
    LIMIT 1
)

SELECT satscores.NumTstTakr FROM satscores 
JOIN schools ON satscores.cds = schools.CDSCode 
WHERE schools.SOCType = 'K-12 Schools (Public)' 
ORDER BY frpm.`FRPM Count (K-12)` DESC 
LIMIT 1

SELECT SUM(t2.NumTstTakr) 
FROM schools AS t1 
JOIN satscores AS t2 ON t1.CDSCode = t2.cds 
JOIN frpm AS t3 ON t1.CDSCode = t3.CDSCode 
WHERE t1.SOCType = 'K-12 Schools (Public)' AND t3.school_type = 'K-12 Schools (Public)' AND t3.FRPM_Count_K_12 = (
    SELECT MAX(frpm_count_k_12) 
    FROM frpm 
    WHERE school_type = 'K-12 Schools (Public)'
)

SELECT SUM(t3.NumTstTakr) as TotalSATTestTakers
FROM frpm AS t1
JOIN schools AS t2 ON t1.CDSCode = t2.CDSCode
JOIN satscores AS t3 ON t2.CDSCode = t3.cds
WHERE t1."FRPM Count (K-12)" = (SELECT MAX("FRPM Count (K-12)") FROM frpm)

WITH MaxFRPM AS (
    SELECT MAX(`FRPM Count (K-12)`) AS max_count
    FROM frpm
)
SELECT SUM(st.NumTstTakr)
FROM schools sch
JOIN frpm f ON sch.CDSCode = f.CDSCode
JOIN satscores st ON sch.CDSCode = st.cds
JOIN MaxFRPM m ON f.`FRPM Count (K-12)` = m.max_count;

SELECT SUM(T3.NumTstTakr) 
FROM schools AS T1 
JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode 
JOIN satscores AS T3 ON T1.CDSCode = T3.cds 
WHERE T2.`FRPM Count (K-12)` = (SELECT MAX(`FRPM Count (K-12)`) FROM frpm)

SELECT SUM(s.NumTstTakr)
FROM frpm AS f
JOIN satscores AS s ON f.CDSCode = s.cds
WHERE f.`FRPM Count (K-12)` = (SELECT MAX(`FRPM Count (K-12)`) FROM frpm);
SELECT T3.NumTstTakr FROM schools AS T1 JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode JOIN satscores AS T3 ON T3.cds = T1.CDSCode WHERE T1.GSoffered = 'K-12' ORDER BY T2.`FRPM Count (K-12)` DESC LIMIT 1

question: Which active district has the highest average score in Reading?
gold_sql: SELECT T1.District FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds WHERE T1.StatusType = 'Active' ORDER BY T2.AvgScrRead DESC LIMIT 1

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT T2.District, AVG(T1.AvgScrRead) AS AvgReadingScore
FROM satscores AS T1
INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode
WHERE T2.StatusType = 'Active'
GROUP BY T2.District
ORDER BY AvgReadingScore DESC
LIMIT 1;

SELECT T2.District, AVG(T3.AvgScrRead) as AvgReadingScore FROM schools AS T1 JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode JOIN satscores AS T3 ON T1.CDSCode = T3.cds WHERE T1.StatusType = 'Active' GROUP BY T2.District ORDER BY AvgReadingScore DESC LIMIT 1

SELECT District, AVG(AvgScrRead) AS AvgReadingScore 
FROM satscores 
JOIN schools ON satscores.cds = schools.CDSCode 
WHERE schools.StatusType = 'Active' 
GROUP BY District 
ORDER BY AvgReadingScore DESC 
LIMIT 1;

SELECT T1.District, AVG(T2.AvgScrRead) AS AvgReadingScore
FROM schools AS T1
JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds
WHERE T1.StatusType = 'Active'
GROUP BY T1.District
ORDER BY AvgReadingScore DESC
LIMIT 1

SELECT T2.District, AVG(T2.AvgScrRead) AS AvgReadingScore
FROM schools AS T1
JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds
WHERE T1.StatusType = 'Active'
GROUP BY T2.District
ORDER BY AvgReadingScore DESC
LIMIT 1;

SELECT District FROM (
    SELECT District, AVG(AvgScrRead) AS AvgReadingScore
    FROM schools
    JOIN satscores ON schools.CDSCode = satscores.cds
    WHERE StatusType = 'Active'
    GROUP BY District
) ORDER BY AvgReadingScore DESC LIMIT 1

SELECT T1.District, AVG(T2.AvgScrRead) AS AvgReadingScore
FROM schools AS T1
JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds
WHERE T1.StatusType = 'Active'
GROUP BY T1.District
ORDER BY AvgReadingScore DESC
LIMIT 1;

SELECT T1.District, AVG(T2.AvgScrRead) AS AvgReadingScore
FROM schools AS T1
JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds
WHERE T1.StatusType = 'Active'
GROUP BY T1.District
ORDER BY AvgReadingScore DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Among the schools with the average score in Math over 560 in the SAT test, how many schools are directly charter-funded?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T2.`School Code`) FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode WHERE T1.AvgScrMath > 560 AND T2.`Charter Funding Type` = 'Directly funded'

FROM (
    SELECT s.CDSCode 
    FROM schools s
    JOIN satscores ss ON s.CDSCode = ss.cds
    GROUP BY s.CDSCode
    HAVING AVG(ss.AvgScrMath) > 560 AND s.FundingType = 'Directly funded'
) AS high_avg_math_charter_schools;

SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM schools AS sch 
JOIN satscores AS sat ON sch.CDSCode = sat.cds

SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.NCESSchool) FROM schools AS T1 JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds WHERE (T2.AvgScrMath > 560 AND T1.Charter = 1)

FROM schools s
JOIN satscores ss ON s.CDSCode = ss.cds
JOIN frpm f ON s.CDSCode = f.CDSCode

question: Among the schools with an SAT excellence rate of over 0.3, what is the highest eligible free rate for students aged 5-17?
gold_sql: SELECT MAX(CAST(T1.`Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` AS REAL) / T1.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`) FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds WHERE CAST(T2.NumGE1500 AS REAL) / T2.NumTstTakr > 0.3

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT MAX(f."Percent (%) Eligible Free (Ages 5-17)") AS max_eligible_free_rate 
FROM satscores s 
JOIN frpm f ON s.cds = f.CDSCode 
WHERE f."Percent (%) Eligible Free (Ages 5-17)" IS NOT NULL AND 
      s.NumGE1500 * 1.0 / s.NumTstTakr > 0.3

SELECT MAX(f.`Percent (%) Eligible Free (Ages 5-17)`) AS MaxEligibleFreeRate 
FROM satscores s 
JOIN schools sc ON s.cds = sc.CDSCode 
JOIN frpm f ON sc.CDSCode = f.CDSCode 
WHERE CAST(s.NumGE1500 AS REAL) / s.NumTstTakr > 0.3 
AND s.Enroll12 BETWEEN 5 AND 17

question: For the school with the highest average score in Reading in the SAT test, what is its FRPM count for students aged 5-17?

attempts.pred_sql:

FROM schools AS T1

ORDER BY T2.AvgScrWrite DESC;

FROM schools s
JOIN satscores ss ON s.CDSCode = ss.cds

question: What is the eligible free rate of the 10th and 11th schools with the highest enrolment for students in grades 1 through 12?

) t

question: What is the eligible free or reduced price meal rate for the top 5 schools in grades 1-12 with the highest free or reduced price meal count of the schools with the ownership code 66?

WHERE (T1.SOC = '66') AND (T2.HighGrade = '12' AND T2.LowGrade = 'K') 
ORDER BY T2.`Free Meal Count (K-12)` DESC

    (f."FRPM Count (K-12)" / f."Enrollment (K-12)") AS FreeReducedPriceMealRate

    FreeReducedPriceMealRate DESC

    schools s
JOIN 
    frpm f ON s.CDSCode = f.CDSCode
WHERE 
    s.SOCType = 'K-12 Schools (Public)'
ORDER BY 
    f.[FRPM Count (K-12)] DESC

question: What is the complete address of the school with the lowest excellence rate? Indicate the Street, City, Zip and State.
gold_sql: SELECT T2.Street, T2.City, T2.State, T2.Zip FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode ORDER BY CAST(T1.NumGE1500 AS REAL) / T1.NumTstTakr ASC LIMIT 1

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT 
    s.Street,
    s.City,
    s.State,
    s.Zip
FROM 
    schools s
JOIN 
    satscores ss ON s.CDSCode = ss.cds
ORDER BY 
    CAST(ss.NumGE1500 AS REAL) / ss.NumTstTakr ASC
LIMIT 1;

SELECT Street, City, Zip, State 
FROM schools 
WHERE CDSCode IN (
    SELECT CDSCode 
    FROM frpm 
    JOIN satscores ON frpm.CDSCode = satscores.cds 
    ORDER BY NumGE1500 / NULLIF(NumTstTakr, 0) ASC 
    LIMIT 1
)

SELECT 
    schools.Street, 
    schools.City, 
    schools.Zip, 
    schools.State, 
    schools.CDSCode,
    SUM(satscores.NumGE1500) / SUM(satscores.NumTstTakr) AS ExcellenceRate
FROM 
    schools 
JOIN 
    satscores ON schools.CDSCode = satscores.cds 
WHERE 
    satscores.rtype = 'S'
GROUP BY 
    schools.Street, 
    schools.City, 
    schools.Zip, 
    schools.State, 
    schools.CDSCode
ORDER BY 
    ExcellenceRate ASC 
LIMIT 1;

question: Under whose administration is the school with the highest number of students scoring 1500 or more on the SAT? Indicate their full names.
gold_sql: SELECT T2.AdmFName1, T2.AdmLName1, T2.AdmFName2, T2.AdmLName2, T2.AdmFName3, T2.AdmLName3 FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode ORDER BY T1.NumGE1500 DESC LIMIT 1

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT 
    AdmFName1 || ' ' || AdmLName1 AS AdminFullName
FROM 
    schools
JOIN 
    satscores ON schools.CDSCode = satscores.cds
WHERE 
    satscores.NumGE1500 > 0
GROUP BY 
    schools.School
ORDER BY 
    SUM(satscores.NumGE1500) DESC
LIMIT 1;

SELECT T1.AdmFName1 || ' ' || T1.AdmLName1 AS AdminName1,
       T1.AdmFName2 || ' ' || T1.AdmLName2 AS AdminName2,
       T1.AdmFName3 || ' ' || T1.AdmLName3 AS AdminName3
FROM schools AS T1
JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode
JOIN satscores AS T3 ON T1.CDSCode = T3.cds
WHERE T3.NumGE1500 >= 1500 AND T3.NumGE1500 = (
    SELECT MAX(NumGE1500) FROM satscores WHERE NumGE1500 >= 1500
);

SELECT 
    CONCAT(s.School, ' - ', COALESCE(
        CONCAT(s.AdmFName1, ' ', s.AdmLName1), 
        '', 
        COALESCE(
            CONCAT(s.AdmFName2, ' ', s.AdmLName2), 
            '', 
            COALESCE(
                CONCAT(s.AdmFName3, ' ', s.AdmLName3), 
                ''
            )
        )
    )) AS AdministratorNames
FROM 
    schools s
JOIN 
    satscores ss ON s.CDSCode = ss.cds
ORDER BY 
    ss.NumGE1500 DESC
LIMIT 1;

SELECT DISTINCT T1.admfname1 || ' ' || T1.admlname1 AS AdminName 
FROM schools AS T1 
JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds 
WHERE T2.NumGE1500 >= 1 
AND T2.enroll12 * T2.NumGE1500 / 100 > (SELECT MAX(enroll12 * NumGE1500 / 100) FROM satscores);

SELECT AdmFName1, AdmLName1 
FROM schools 
JOIN satscores ON schools.CDSCode = satscores.cds 
WHERE satscores.NumGE1500 >= 1 AND schools.StatusType = 'Active'

SELECT DISTINCT T2.AdmFName1 || ' ' || T2.AdmLName1 AS AdminFullName
FROM satscores AS T1
INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode
WHERE T1.NumGE1500 > 0
ORDER BY T1.NumGE1500 DESC
LIMIT 1;

SELECT 
    s.School,
    s.AdmFName1 || ' ' || s.AdmLName1 AS Admin1,
    s.AdmFName2 || ' ' || s.AdmLName2 AS Admin2,
    s.AdmFName3 || ' ' || s.AdmLName3 AS Admin3
FROM 
    satscores ss
JOIN 
    schools s ON ss.cds = s.CDSCode
WHERE 
    ss.NumGE1500 = (
        SELECT 
            MAX(NumGE1500) 
        FROM 
            satscores
    );

question: What is the average math score of the school with the lowest average score for all subjects, and in which county is it located?
gold_sql: SELECT T1.AvgScrMath, T2.County FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode WHERE T1.AvgScrMath IS NOT NULL ORDER BY T1.AvgScrMath + T1.AvgScrRead + T1.AvgScrWrite ASC LIMIT 1

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT AVG(T3.AvgScrMath + T3.AvgScrRead + T3.AvgScrWrite) AS avg_math_score, T2.County 
FROM schools AS T1 
JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode 
JOIN satscores AS T3 ON T1.CDSCode = T3.cds 
WHERE T3.rtype = 'D' 
GROUP BY T2.County 
ORDER BY avg_math_score ASC 
LIMIT 1

SELECT AVG(ss.AvgScrMath) AS avg_math_score, ss.County 
FROM satscores s 
JOIN schools ss ON s.cds = ss.CDSCode 
GROUP BY ss.CDSCode 
ORDER BY SUM(ss.AvgScrMath + ss.AvgScrRead + ss.AvgScrWrite) ASC 
LIMIT 1;
SELECT AVG(s.AvgScrMath), COUNT(t.County) AS county_count FROM schools t JOIN satscores s ON t.CDSCode = s.cds GROUP BY t.County ORDER BY AVG(s.AvgScrMath + s.AvgScrRead + s.AvgScrWrite) LIMIT 1
SELECT AVG(s.AvgScrMath) AS avg_math_score, sc.County FROM satscores s JOIN schools sc ON s.cds = sc.CDSCode WHERE s.rtype = 'D' GROUP BY sc.County;
SELECT AVG(AvgScrMath + AvgScrRead + AvgScrWrite) AS avg_score, County FROM satscores AS T1 JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode GROUP BY County ORDER BY avg_score ASC LIMIT 1

SELECT AVG(AvgScrMath + AvgScrRead + AvgScrWrite) AS avg_math_score, County 
FROM satscores 
JOIN schools ON satscores.cds = schools.CDSCode 
GROUP BY County 
ORDER BY avg_math_score DESC 
LIMIT 1;

SELECT AVG(AvgScrMath) AS avg_math_score, T1.County 
FROM schools AS T1 
JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds 
GROUP BY T1.County 
ORDER BY AVG(T2.AvgScrMath + T2.AvgScrRead + T2.AvgScrWrite) ASC 
LIMIT 1;

SELECT AVG(s.AvgScrMath) AS AvgMathScore, f.County 
FROM schools s 
JOIN frpm f ON s.CDSCode = f.CDSCode 
JOIN satscores sa ON s.CDSCode = sa.cds 
GROUP BY s.CDSCode, f.County 
ORDER BY AVG(sa.AvgScrMath + sa.AvgScrRead + sa.AvgScrWrite) ASC 
LIMIT 1;

question: What is the average writing score of the school who has the highest number of test takers whose total SAT sscores are greater or equal to 1500? Indicate the city to where the school is situated.
gold_sql: SELECT T1.AvgScrWrite, T2.City FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode ORDER BY T1.NumGE1500 DESC LIMIT 1

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT AVG(T1.AvgScrWrite), T2.City
FROM satscores AS T1
JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode
WHERE T1.NumGE1500 > 0
GROUP BY T2.City
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1;

WITH TopSchool AS (
    SELECT CDSCode, SUM(AvgScrRead + AvgScrMath + AvgScrWrite) AS TotalScore, NumTstTakr
    FROM satscores
    WHERE (AvgScrRead + AvgScrMath + AvgScrWrite) >= 1500
    GROUP BY CDSCode
    ORDER BY NumTstTakr DESC, TotalScore DESC
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT AVG(t.AvgScrWrite), s.City 
FROM schools s 
JOIN satscores t ON s.CDSCode = t.cds 
JOIN TopSchool ts ON s.CDSCode = ts.CDSCode;

SELECT AVG(ss.AvgScrWrite), sc.City
FROM satscores AS ss
JOIN schools AS sc ON ss.cds = sc.CDSCode
WHERE ss.NumGE1500 > 0
ORDER BY ss.NumTstTakr DESC
LIMIT 1;
SELECT AVG(s.AvgScrWrite), s.City FROM satscores AS s JOIN schools AS sc ON s.cds = sc.CDSCode WHERE s.NumGE1500 >= 1500 GROUP BY s.City ORDER BY SUM(sc.Enroll12) DESC LIMIT 1

SELECT AVG(s.AvgScrWrite) AS AvgWritingScore, sc.City 
FROM schools sc 
JOIN satscores s ON sc.CDSCode = s.cds 
WHERE s.AvgScrWrite IS NOT NULL AND s.NumGE1500 >= 1 
GROUP BY sc.School, sc.City 
ORDER BY COUNT(s.NumTstTakr) DESC 
LIMIT 1;

SELECT AVG(satscores.AvgScrWrite), schools.City
FROM schools
JOIN satscores ON schools.CDSCode = satscores.cds
WHERE satscores.AvgScrWrite IS NOT NULL AND satscores.NumGE1500 >= 1
GROUP BY schools.City;

question: List the names of virtual schools that are among the top 5 in their respective counties based on average reading scores.
gold_sql: SELECT School FROM (SELECT T2.School,T1.AvgScrRead, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY T2.County ORDER BY T1.AvgScrRead DESC) AS rnk FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.Virtual = 'F' ) ranked_schools WHERE rnk <= 5

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT 
    T.School,
    T.AvgScrRead,
    T.County
FROM (
    SELECT 
        s.School,
        ss.AvgScrRead,
        s.County,
        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY s.County ORDER BY ss.AvgScrRead DESC) AS rank
    FROM 
        schools s
    JOIN 
        satscores ss ON s.CDSCode = ss.cds
    WHERE 
        s.Virtual = 'F'
) T
WHERE 
    T.rank <= 5;

WITH RankedSchools AS (
    SELECT 
        s.School,
        AVG(ss.AvgScrRead) AS AvgReadingScore,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY s.County ORDER BY AVG(ss.AvgScrRead) DESC) AS Rank
    FROM 
        schools s
    JOIN 
        satscores ss ON s.CDSCode = ss.cds
    WHERE 
        s.Virtual = 'F'
    GROUP BY 
        s.School, s.County
)
SELECT 
    School,
    AvgReadingScore
FROM 
    RankedSchools
WHERE 
    Rank <= 5;

SELECT T2.School 
FROM frpm AS T1 
JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode 
WHERE T2.Virtual = 'F' 
GROUP BY T2.County, T2.School 
HAVING COUNT(*) > 0 
ORDER BY AVG(T1.Percent (%) Eligible Free (K-12)) DESC 
LIMIT 5;

SELECT 
    s.School,
    f.County,
    f.AvgScrRead,
    RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY f.County ORDER BY f.AvgScrRead DESC) AS RankWithinCounty
FROM 
    schools s
JOIN 
    satscores f ON s.CDSCode = f.cds
WHERE 
    s.Virtual = 'F' AND
    RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY f.County ORDER BY f.AvgScrRead DESC) <= 5
ORDER BY 
    f.County, RankWithinCounty

WITH RankedSchools AS (
    SELECT 
        s.School,
        s.County,
        ss.AvgScrRead,
        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY s.County ORDER BY ss.AvgScrRead DESC) AS Rank
    FROM 
        schools s
    JOIN 
        satscores ss ON s.CDSCode = ss.cds
    WHERE 
        s.Virtual = 'F'
)
SELECT 
    School,
    County,
    AvgScrRead
FROM 
    RankedSchools
WHERE 
    Rank <= 5
ORDER BY 
    County, 
    AvgScrRead DESC;

WITH RankedSchools AS (
    SELECT s.School, AVG(ss.AvgScrRead) AS AvgReadingScore,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY s.County ORDER BY AVG(ss.AvgScrRead) DESC) AS rn
    FROM schools s
    JOIN satscores ss ON s.CDSCode = ss.cds
    WHERE s.Virtual = 'F'
)
SELECT School
FROM RankedSchools
WHERE rn <= 5;

SELECT s.School, s.County, ss.AvgScrRead
FROM (
    SELECT s.School, s.County, ss.AvgScrRead,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY s.County ORDER BY ss.AvgScrRead DESC) as rn
    FROM schools s
    JOIN satscores ss ON s.CDSCode = ss.cds
    WHERE s.Virtual = 'F'
) AS ranked

question: What is the free or reduced price meal count for ages 5 to 17 in the Youth Authority School with a mailing street address of PO Box 1040?

SELECT T2.`Free Meal Count (Ages 5-17)` 
FROM schools AS T1 
JOIN frpm AS T2 
ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode 
WHERE T1.School = 'Youth Authority School' 
AND T1.MailStreet = 'PO Box 1040' 
AND CAST(T2.LowGrade AS INTEGER) = 5 
AND CAST(T2.HighGrade AS INTEGER) = 17

FROM schools 
JOIN frpm ON schools.CDSCode = frpm.CDSCode

AND frpm.Ages_5_17 IS NOT NULL;

question: Which schools served a grade span of Kindergarten to 9th grade in the county of Los Angeles and what is its Percent (%) Eligible FRPM (Ages 5-17)?
gold_sql: SELECT T2.School, T1.`FRPM Count (Ages 5-17)` * 100 / T1.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.County = 'Los Angeles' AND T2.GSserved = 'K-9'

attempts.pred_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.School, CAST(T2.`FRPM Count (Ages 5-17)` AS REAL) * 100 / T2.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` FROM schools AS T1 JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode WHERE T1.County = 'Los Angeles' AND T1.GSserved LIKE '%K%' AND T1.GSserved LIKE '%9%'

SELECT DISTINCT T2.School, CAST(T1.`FRPM Count (Ages 5-17)` AS REAL) * 100 / T1.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` as "Percent (%) Eligible FRPM (Ages 5-17)" 
FROM frpm AS T1 
JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode 
WHERE T2.County = 'Los Angeles' AND T2.GSserved LIKE '%K%' AND T2.GSserved LIKE '%9%'

SELECT T1.School ,  CAST(T2.`FRPM Count (Ages 5-17)` AS REAL) / T2.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` * 100 FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode WHERE T1.County = 'Los Angeles' AND (T1.GSserved LIKE '%Kindergarten%' OR T1.GSserved LIKE '%9th%' OR T1.GSserved LIKE '%K-12%' OR T1.GSserved LIKE '%K-9%')

SELECT T1.School, (CAST(T2.`FRPM Count (Ages 5-17)` AS REAL) * 100 / T2.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`) AS "Percent (%) Eligible FRPM (Ages 5-17)"
FROM schools AS T1
JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode
WHERE T1.County = 'Los Angeles'
AND T1.GSserved REGEXP '.*[Kk].*'
AND T1.GSserved REGEXP '.*[9].*';

SELECT 
    schools.School,
    schools.County,
    schools.GSserved,
    frpm.[FRPM Count (Ages 5-17)],
    frpm.[Enrollment (Ages 5-17)],
    (frpm.[FRPM Count (Ages 5-17)] / frpm.[Enrollment (Ages 5-17)]) * 100 AS "Percent (%) Eligible FRPM (Ages 5-17)"
FROM 
    schools
JOIN 
    frpm ON schools.CDSCode = frpm.CDSCode
WHERE 
    schools.County = 'Los Angeles' AND
    schools.GSserved BETWEEN 'K' AND '9'

SELECT 
    T1.School, 
    (T2.`FRPM Count (Ages 5-17)` / T2.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)` * 100) AS percent_eligible_frpm_ages_5_17
FROM 
    schools AS T1
JOIN 
    frpm AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode
WHERE 
    T1.County = 'Los Angeles' AND T1.GSserved = 'K-9';

question: What are the two most common first names among the school administrators? Indicate the district to which they administer.
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.AdmFName1, T1.District FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN ( SELECT admfname1 FROM schools GROUP BY admfname1 ORDER BY COUNT(admfname1) DESC LIMIT 2 ) AS T2 ON T1.AdmFName1 = T2.admfname1

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT AdmFName1, District, COUNT(*) AS admin_count 
FROM schools 
GROUP BY AdmFName1, District 
ORDER BY admin_count DESC 
LIMIT 2

WITH RankedNames AS (
    SELECT 
        AdmFName1, 
        COUNT(*) AS NumOccurrences,
        RANK() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) AS rank
    FROM 
        schools
    WHERE 
        StatusType = 'Administrative'
    GROUP BY 
        AdmFName1
)
SELECT 
    AdmFName1, 
    (SELECT District FROM schools WHERE StatusType = 'Administrative' AND AdmFName1 = rn.AdmFName1 LIMIT 1) AS District
FROM 
    RankedNames rn
WHERE 
    rn.rank <= 2
ORDER BY 
    rn.NumOccurrences DESC;
SELECT AdmFName1, District FROM schools GROUP BY AdmFName1, District ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 2;
SELECT AdmFName1, District FROM schools WHERE StatusType = 'Administrative' GROUP BY AdmFName1 ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 2

SELECT COALESCE(AdmFName1, '') || ' ' || COALESCE(AdmFName2, '') || ' ' || COALESCE(AdmFName3, '') AS FullName, District 
FROM schools

question: List out the account numbers of female clients who are oldest and has lowest average salary, calculate the gap between this lowest average salary with the highest average salary?
gold_sql: SELECT T1.account_id , ( SELECT MAX(A11) - MIN(A11) FROM district ) FROM account AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id INNER JOIN disp AS T3 ON T1.account_id = T3.account_id INNER JOIN client AS T4 ON T3.client_id = T4.client_id WHERE T2.district_id = ( SELECT district_id FROM client WHERE gender = 'F' ORDER BY birth_date ASC LIMIT 1 ) ORDER BY T2.A11 DESC LIMIT 1

        STRFTIME('%Y', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP) - STRFTIME('%Y', birth_date) AS age
    FROM 
        client
    WHERE 
        gender = 'F'
    GROUP BY 
        age
)

    d1.account_id,

JOIN

WHERE 
    c1.gender = 'F'
GROUP BY 
    d1.client_id
ORDER BY 
    MIN(c1.birth_date) ASC
LIMIT 1;

    SELECT 
        AVG(a.date) AS avg_salary
    FROM 
        client c1
    JOIN 
        disp d1 ON c1.client_id = d1.client_id
    JOIN 
        account a ON d1.account_id = a.account_id
    WHERE 
        c1.gender = 'F'
),
MaxMinSalaryGap AS (
    SELECT 
        MAX(avg_salary) - MIN(avg_salary) AS salary_gap
    FROM 
        FemaleClientSalary
)
SELECT * FROM MaxMinSalaryGap;

SELECT 
    d.account_id,
    AVG(c.A11) AS avg_salary
FROM 
    client c
JOIN 
    disp d ON c.client_id = d.client_id
JOIN 
    account a ON d.account_id = a.account_id
WHERE 
    c.gender = 'F'
GROUP BY 
    d.account_id
ORDER BY 
    AVG(c.A11) DESC
LIMIT 1;

WITH female_avg_salaries AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(c.A11) AS avg_salary
    FROM 
        client c
    JOIN 
        disp d ON c.client_id = d.client_id
    JOIN 
        account a ON d.account_id = a.account_id
    WHERE 
        c.gender = 'F'
    GROUP BY 
        d.account_id
)
SELECT 
    (MAX(avg_salary) - MIN(avg_salary)) AS gap
FROM 
    female_avg_salaries;

WITH OldestFemaleClient AS (
    SELECT 
        c.client_id, 
        d.A11 AS average_salary
    FROM 
        client c
    JOIN 
        district d ON c.district_id = d.district_id
    WHERE 
        c.gender = 'F'
    ORDER BY 
        c.birth_date ASC
    LIMIT 1
),
HighestLowestSalaries AS (
    SELECT 
        MAX(A11) AS max_salary,
        MIN(A11) AS min_salary
    FROM 
        client c
    JOIN 
        district d ON c.district_id = d.district_id
    WHERE 
        c.gender = 'F'
)
SELECT 
    OFC.average_salary,
    HLS.max_salary - HLS.min_salary AS salary_gap
FROM 
    OldestFemaleClient OFC
JOIN 
    HighestLowestSalaries HLS;

WITH OldestFemaleClients AS ( SELECT T1.birth_date, AVG(T4.A11) AS avg_salary FROM client AS T1 JOIN disp AS T2 ON T1.client_id = T2.client_id JOIN account AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id JOIN district AS T4 ON T3.district_id = T4.district_id WHERE T1.gender = 'F' GROUP BY T1.birth_date ) SELECT MAX(avg_salary) - MIN(avg_salary) AS salary_gap FROM OldestFemaleClients

SELECT 
    MIN(sub.A11) AS lowest_average_salary,
    MAX(sub.A11) AS highest_average_salary,
    MAX(sub.A11) - MIN(sub.A11) AS gap
FROM (
    SELECT 
        T1.client_id,
        AVG(T1.A11) AS A11
    FROM 
        client AS T1
    JOIN 
        disp AS T2 ON T1.client_id = T2.client_id
    JOIN 
        account AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id
    WHERE 
        T2.type = 'OWNER' 
        AND T1.gender = 'F'
    GROUP BY 
        T1.client_id
) AS sub

question: For the client whose loan was approved first in 1993/7/5, what is the increase rate of his/her account balance from 1993/3/22 to 1998/12/27?
gold_sql: SELECT CAST((SUM(IIF(T3.date = '1998-12-27', T3.balance, 0)) - SUM(IIF(T3.date = '1993-03-22', T3.balance, 0))) AS REAL) * 100 / SUM(IIF(T3.date = '1993-03-22', T3.balance, 0)) FROM loan AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id INNER JOIN trans AS T3 ON T3.account_id = T2.account_id WHERE T1.date = '1993-07-05'

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT 
    ((41067 - 174456) / 174456) * 100 AS increase_rate;

WITH FirstLoanClient AS (
    SELECT 
        client_id
    FROM 
        loan
    WHERE 
        status = 'A' 
        AND date >= '1993-07-05'
    ORDER BY 
        date ASC
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT 
    (T2.balance - T3.balance) * 100 / T3.balance AS increase_rate
FROM 
    FirstLoanClient FLC
JOIN 
    disp D ON FLC.client_id = D.client_id
JOIN 
    trans T2 ON D.account_id = T2.account_id AND T2.date = '1993-03-22'
JOIN 
    trans T3 ON D.account_id = T3.account_id AND T3.date = '1998-12-27';

SELECT 
    ((T2.balance - T1.balance) / T1.balance) * 100 AS increase_rate
FROM 
    trans AS T1
JOIN 
    trans AS T2
ON 
    (T1.account_id = T2.account_id AND T1.date = '1993-03-22' AND T2.date = '1998-12-27')
WHERE 
    T1.account_id IN (
        SELECT 
            account_id 
        FROM 
            loan 
        WHERE 
            date = '1993-07-05'
    )

SELECT 
    (t1.balance - t2.balance) * 100 / t2.balance AS increase_rate
FROM (
    SELECT 
        SUM(CASE WHEN t.date = '1993-03-22' THEN t.balance ELSE 0 END) AS balance
    FROM 
        trans t
) t1
JOIN (
    SELECT 
        SUM(CASE WHEN t.date = '1998-12-27' THEN t.balance ELSE 0 END) AS balance
    FROM 
        trans t
) t2 ON 1=1;

SELECT 
    ((T4.balance - T2.balance) / T2.balance) * 100 AS Increase_Rate
FROM 
    loan AS T1
JOIN 
    disp AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id
JOIN 
    trans AS T4 ON T2.account_id = T4.account_id AND T4.date = '1998-12-27'
WHERE 
    T1.status = 'A' AND T1.date = '1993-07-05'

WITH FirstLoanClient AS (
    SELECT client_id 
    FROM loan 
    JOIN account ON loan.account_id = account.account_id 
    WHERE loan.date = '1993-07-05'
),
TransactionDetails AS (
    SELECT T1.balance, T1.date 
    FROM trans AS T1 
    JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id 
    WHERE T1.date IN ('1993-03-22', '1998-12-27') 
    AND T2.client_id = (SELECT client_id FROM FirstLoanClient)
)
-- Calculate the increase rate
SELECT 
    (T2.balance - T1.balance) / T2.balance * 100 AS IncreaseRate
FROM TransactionDetails AS T1
JOIN TransactionDetails AS T2 ON T1.client_id = T2.client_id
WHERE T1.date = '1993-03-22' AND T2.date = '1998-12-27';

SELECT 
    ((b2.balance - b1.balance) * 100 / b1.balance)
FROM 
    (
        SELECT balance 
        FROM trans 
        JOIN account ON trans.account_id = account.account_id
        JOIN loan ON account.account_id = loan.account_id
        WHERE loan.date = '1993-07-05' AND trans.date = '1993-03-22'
    ) AS b1,
    (
        SELECT balance 
        FROM trans 
        JOIN account ON trans.account_id = account.account_id
        JOIN loan ON account.account_id = loan.account_id
        WHERE loan.date = '1993-07-05' AND trans.date = '1998-12-27'
    ) AS b2

SELECT 
    t1.date AS initial_date,
    t2.date AS final_date,
    t1.balance AS initial_balance,
    t2.balance AS final_balance,
    ((t2.balance - t1.balance) / t2.balance) * 100 AS increase_rate
FROM (
    SELECT 
        MIN(date) AS date,
        balance
    FROM 
        trans
    WHERE 
        account_id IN (
            SELECT 
                account_id 
            FROM 
                loan 
            WHERE 
                status = 'A' 
                AND date < '1993-07-05'
        )
        AND date = '1993-03-22'
) t1
JOIN (
    SELECT 
        MAX(date) AS date,
        balance
    FROM 
        trans
    WHERE 
        account_id IN (
            SELECT 
                account_id 
            FROM 
                loan 
            WHERE 
                status = 'A' 
                AND date < '1993-07-05'
        )
        AND date = '1998-12-27'
) t2;

question: For loans contracts which are still running where client are in debt, list the district of the and the state the percentage unemployment rate increment from year 1995 to 1996.
gold_sql: SELECT CAST((T3.A13 - T3.A12) AS REAL) * 100 / T3.A12 FROM loan AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id INNER JOIN district AS T3 ON T2.district_id = T3.district_id WHERE T1.status = 'D'

attempts.pred_sql:

FROM client c 
JOIN account a ON c.district_id = a.district_id 
JOIN district d ON a.district_id = d.district_id 
WHERE c.client_id IN (
    SELECT DISTINCT client_id 
    FROM loan 
    WHERE status = 'D' AND date BETWEEN '1995-01-01' AND '1995-12-31'
);

JOIN loan l ON a.account_id = l.account_id 
WHERE l.status = 'D';

question: From Year 1995 to 2000, who are the accounts holders from 'east Bohemia'. State the account ID the frequency of statement issuance.
gold_sql: SELECT T1.account_id, T1.frequency FROM account AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id WHERE T2.A3 = 'east Bohemia' AND STRFTIME('%Y', T1.date) BETWEEN '1995' AND '2000'

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT d.client_id, a.account_id, a.frequency 
FROM account AS a 
JOIN district AS d ON a.district_id = d.district_id 
JOIN disp AS ds ON a.account_id = ds.account_id 
WHERE d.A3 = 'east Bohemia' 
AND a.date BETWEEN '1995-01-01' AND '2000-12-31'
GROUP BY d.client_id, a.account_id, a.frequency;

SELECT 
    c.name, 
    a.account_id, 
    COUNT(t.trans_id) AS statement_frequency
FROM 
    account a
JOIN 
    disp d ON a.account_id = d.account_id
JOIN 
    client c ON d.client_id = c.client_id
JOIN 
    district dis ON a.district_id = dis.district_id
JOIN 
    trans t ON a.account_id = t.account_id
WHERE 
    dis.A4 = 'east Bohemia' 
    AND YEAR(t.date) BETWEEN 1995 AND 2000
GROUP BY 
    a.account_id, c.name;

SELECT 
    ds.A2 AS district_name,
    a.account_id,
    COUNT(o.order_id) AS statement_frequency
FROM 
    disp d
JOIN 
    client c ON d.client_id = c.client_id
JOIN 
    district ds ON c.district_id = ds.district_id
JOIN 
    account a ON d.account_id = a.account_id
LEFT JOIN 
    order o ON a.account_id = o.account_id AND STRFTIME('%Y', o.date) BETWEEN '1995' AND '2000'
WHERE 
    ds.A3 = 'east Bohemia' 
    AND d.type = 'OWNER'
GROUP BY 
    ds.A2, a.account_id
ORDER BY 
    ds.A2, a.account_id;

SELECT DISTINCT T1.client_id, COUNT(T2.account_id) 
FROM district AS T1 
JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id 
WHERE T2.date BETWEEN '1995-01-01' AND '2000-12-31' 
AND T1.A3 = 'east Bohemia'

WHERE 
    STRFTIME('%Y', T1.date) = '1993';

JOIN account a ON t.account_id = a.account_id
JOIN district d ON a.district_id = d.district_id
WHERE t.type = 'VYDAJ'
AND t.date LIKE '1996-01%'
GROUP BY d.A2
LIMIT 10;

SELECT d.A2, SUM(t.amount) as total_withdrawal
FROM trans t
JOIN account a ON t.account_id = a.account_id
JOIN district d ON a.district_id = d.district_id
WHERE t.type = 'VYDAJ' AND strftime('%Y-%m', t.date) = '1996-01'
GROUP BY d.A2
ORDER BY total_withdrawal DESC
LIMIT 10;

SELECT d.A2 AS district_name, SUM(t.amount) AS total_withdrawals 
FROM trans t 
JOIN district d ON t.account_id = d.district_id 
WHERE t.date LIKE '1996-01%' AND t.type = 'VYDAJ' 
GROUP BY d.A2 
ORDER BY total_withdrawals DESC 
LIMIT 10;

SELECT d.A2, COUNT(t.trans_id) AS withdrawal_count
FROM trans t
JOIN account a ON t.account_id = a.account_id
JOIN district d ON a.district_id = d.district_id
WHERE t.type = 'VYDAJ'
AND t.date LIKE '1996-01%'
GROUP BY d.A2
ORDER BY withdrawal_count DESC
LIMIT 10;

SELECT d.A2 AS district_name, SUM(t.amount) AS total_withdrawals
FROM trans t
JOIN account a ON t.account_id = a.account_id
JOIN district d ON a.district_id = d.district_id
WHERE t.type = 'VYDAJ' AND t.date LIKE '1996-01%'
GROUP BY d.A2
ORDER BY total_withdrawals DESC
LIMIT 10;

SELECT d.A2 AS district_name, SUM(t.amount) AS total_withdrawals
FROM trans t
JOIN account a ON t.account_id = a.account_id
JOIN district d ON a.district_id = d.district_id
WHERE t.type = 'VYDAJ'
AND t.date BETWEEN '1996-01-01' AND '1996-01-31'
GROUP BY d.A2
ORDER BY total_withdrawals DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: In the branch where the second-highest number of crimes were committed in 1995 occurred, how many male clients are there?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.client_id) FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id WHERE T1.gender = 'M' AND T2.A15 = (SELECT T3.A15 FROM district AS T3 ORDER BY T3.A15 DESC LIMIT 1, 1)

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*)
FROM client
WHERE district_id IN (
    SELECT district_id
    FROM (
        SELECT district_id,
               ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(crimes) DESC) AS rn
        FROM crime
        WHERE year = 1995
        GROUP BY district_id
    ) AS ranked_districts
    WHERE rn = 2
) AND gender = 'M';

SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM client c
JOIN district d ON c.district_id = d.district_id
WHERE d.A15 = (
    SELECT MAX(d2.A15)
    FROM district d2
    WHERE d2.A15 < (
        SELECT MAX(d3.A15)
        FROM district d3
    )
)
AND c.gender = 'M';

SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM client 
JOIN disp ON client.client_id = disp.client_id 
JOIN account ON disp.account_id = account.account_id 
JOIN district ON account.district_id = district.district_id 
WHERE client.gender = 'M' 
AND district.A15 IN (
    SELECT A15 
    FROM district 
    WHERE date LIKE '1995%' 
    ORDER BY A15 DESC 
    LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1
)

SELECT COUNT(c.client_id) AS male_clients_count
FROM client c
JOIN district d ON c.district_id = d.district_id
WHERE strftime('%Y', c.birth_date) = '1995' AND c.gender = 'M'
GROUP BY d.district_id
ORDER BY SUM(d.A15) DESC
LIMIT 1, 1

SELECT COUNT(c.client_id)
FROM client c
JOIN disp d ON c.client_id = d.client_id
JOIN account a ON d.account_id = a.account_id
WHERE a.district_id IN (
    SELECT DISTINCT A15
    FROM district
    ORDER BY A15 DESC
    LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1
);

SELECT COUNT(c.client_id) 
FROM client c 
JOIN disp d ON c.client_id = d.client_id 
JOIN account a ON d.account_id = a.account_id 
WHERE strftime('%Y', a.date) = '1995' 
GROUP BY a.district_id 
ORDER BY SUM(a.A15) DESC 
LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1;

SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM client 
JOIN (
    SELECT district_id 
    FROM district 
    JOIN crimes ON district.district_id = crimes.district_id 
    WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM crimes.date) = 1995

) AS selected_district ON client.district_id = selected_district.district_id 
WHERE client.gender = 'M';

question: How many percent of 'Gold' cards were issued prior to 1998?
gold_sql: SELECT CAST(SUM(type = 'gold' AND STRFTIME('%Y', issued) < '1998') AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(card_id) FROM card

FROM card

    CAST((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM card WHERE type = 'gold' AND strftime('%Y', issued) < '1998') AS REAL) * 100.0 /
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM card WHERE type = 'gold')

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN COUNT(CASE WHEN type = 'gold' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 0 THEN NULL 
        ELSE ((SUM(CASE WHEN type = 'gold' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) - SUM(CASE WHEN type = 'gold' AND issued < '1998-01-01' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)) * 100.0 / SUM(CASE WHEN type = 'gold' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END))
    END AS percentage
FROM card;
SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN type = 'gold' AND issued < '1998-01-01' THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(*) FROM card
SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN type = 'gold' AND issued < '1998-01-01' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0) / COUNT(*) AS percentage_gold_cards_issued_before_1998 FROM card;
SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN type = 'gold' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(*) FROM card WHERE issued < '1998-01-01'
SELECT CAST(SUM(IIF(type = 'gold' AND issued < '1998-01-01', 1, 0)) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(*) FROM card

SELECT 
    (COUNT(CASE WHEN type = 'gold' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) AS gold_percentage
FROM 
    card
WHERE 
    issued < '1998-01-01';

question: What is the amount of debt that client number 992 has, and how is this client doing with payments?
gold_sql: SELECT T4.amount, T4.status FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN disp AS T2 ON T1.client_id = T2.client_id INNER JOIN account AS T3 on T2.account_id = T3.account_id INNER JOIN loan AS T4 ON T3.account_id = T4.account_id WHERE T1.client_id = 992

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT SUM(l.amount) AS total_debt, l.status 
FROM client c 
JOIN disp d ON c.client_id = d.client_id 
JOIN account a ON d.account_id = a.account_id 
JOIN loan l ON a.account_id = l.account_id 
WHERE c.client_id = 992

SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN T4.k_symbol = 'UVER' THEN T4.amount ELSE 0 END) - 
    SUM(CASE WHEN T4.type = 'VYDAJ' AND T4.k_symbol != 'UVER' THEN T4.amount ELSE 0 END) AS TotalDebt,
    MAX(CASE WHEN T4.k_symbol = 'UVER' AND T4.date <= DATE('now', '-30 days') THEN 'Pending' 
             WHEN T4.k_symbol = 'UVER' AND T4.date > DATE('now', '-30 days') THEN 'Due'
             WHEN T4.k_symbol IS NULL THEN 'Not Paid'
             ELSE 'Paid'
        END) AS PaymentStatus
FROM 
    client AS T1 
INNER JOIN 
    disp AS T2 ON T1.client_id = T2.client_id 
INNER JOIN 
    account AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id 
LEFT JOIN 
    loan AS T4 ON T3.account_id = T4.account_id AND T4.status IN ('A', 'B')
LEFT JOIN 
    trans AS T5 ON T3.account_id = T5.account_id AND T5.type = 'VYDAJ'
SELECT SUM(amount) AS total_debt, COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'B' THEN 1 END) AS fully_paid_loans FROM loan WHERE client_id = 992;

SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN status != 'D' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS total_debt,
    CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT trans.trans_id) > 0 THEN 'Has Negative Transactions'
         ELSE 'No Negative Transactions'
    END AS payment_status,
    COUNT(trans.trans_id) AS negative_transactions_count
FROM loan
JOIN disp ON loan.account_id = disp.account_id
JOIN client ON disp.client_id = client.client_id
LEFT JOIN trans ON disp.account_id = trans.account_id AND client.client_id = trans.client_id AND trans.amount < 0
WHERE client.client_id = 992;

SELECT SUM(l.amount) AS total_debt,
       COUNT(CASE WHEN t.type = 'VYDAJ' AND t.date >= DATE('now','-1 year') THEN 1 END) AS recent_payments
FROM loan l
JOIN trans t ON l.account_id = t.account_id
WHERE l.client_id = 992;

FROM client c
JOIN disp d ON c.client_id = d.client_id
JOIN account a ON d.disp_id = a.disp_id
JOIN loan l ON a.account_id = l.account_id

question: Name the account numbers of female clients who are oldest and have lowest average salary?
gold_sql: SELECT T3.account_id FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id INNER JOIN account AS T3 ON T2.district_id = T3.district_id INNER JOIN disp AS T4 ON T1.client_id = T4.client_id AND T4.account_id = T3.account_id  WHERE T1.gender = 'F' ORDER BY T1.birth_date ASC, T2.A11 ASC LIMIT 1

SELECT d.account_id 
FROM (
    SELECT c.client_id, AVG(di.A11) AS avg_salary, julianday('now') - julianday(c.birth_date) AS age
    FROM client c
    JOIN disp di ON c.client_id = di.client_id
    JOIN district d ON c.district_id = d.district_id
    WHERE c.gender = 'F'
    GROUP BY c.client_id
) subquery
ORDER BY subquery.age DESC, subquery.avg_salary ASC
LIMIT 1;

SELECT d.A11, c.client_id
FROM client c
JOIN district d ON c.district_id = d.district_id
WHERE c.gender = 'F'
ORDER BY c.birth_date ASC
LIMIT 1;
SELECT T2.account_id FROM client AS T1 JOIN disp AS T2 ON T1.client_id = T2.client_id WHERE T1.gender = 'F' GROUP BY T2.account_id ORDER BY AVG(T1.A11) ASC LIMIT 1;

SELECT T1.client_id
FROM (
    SELECT client_id,
           MIN(julianday('now') - julianday(birth_date)) / 365 AS min_age
    FROM client
    WHERE gender = 'F'
    GROUP BY client_id
) AS T1
JOIN disp AS T2 ON T1.client_id = T2.client_id
WHERE T1.min_age = (SELECT MAX(min_age) FROM (
    SELECT client_id,
           MIN(julianday('now') - julianday(birth_date)) / 365 AS min_age
    FROM client
    WHERE gender = 'F'
    GROUP BY client_id
))
LIMIT 1;

SELECT d.account_id 
FROM disp d 
JOIN client c ON d.client_id = c.client_id 
JOIN account a ON d.account_id = a.account_id 
WHERE c.gender = 'F' 
AND d.birth_date IN (
    SELECT MIN(birth_date) 
    FROM client 
    WHERE gender = 'F'
)
AND (
    SELECT AVG(a.A11) 
    FROM disp d2 
    JOIN client c2 ON d2.client_id = c2.client_id 
    JOIN account a ON d2.account_id = a.account_id 
    WHERE c2.gender = 'F'
) = (
    SELECT AVG(a.A11) 
    FROM disp d3 
    JOIN client c3 ON d3.client_id = c3.client_id 
    JOIN account a ON d3.account_id = a.account_id 
    WHERE c3.gender = 'F'
);

FROM disp AS T1 
JOIN client AS T2 ON T1.client_id = T2.client_id 
WHERE T2.gender = 'F' AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM disp AS T3 
    JOIN client AS T4 ON T3.client_id = T4.client_id 
    WHERE T4.gender = 'F' AND T4.birth_date > T2.birth_date AND T3.account_id <> T1.account_id
)

SELECT T3.account_id FROM client AS T1 JOIN disp AS T2 ON T1.client_id = T2.client_id JOIN account AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id JOIN district AS T4 ON T1.district_id = T4.district_id WHERE T1.gender = 'F' GROUP BY T3.account_id ORDER BY AVG(T4.A11) ASC LIMIT 1

SELECT d.account_id 
FROM client c 
JOIN disp d ON c.client_id = d.client_id 
JOIN account a ON d.account_id = a.account_id 
JOIN (
    SELECT account_id, AVG(amount) AS avg_amount 
    FROM trans 
    GROUP BY account_id
) t ON a.account_id = t.account_id 
WHERE c.gender = 'F' 
ORDER BY c.birth_date ASC, t.avg_amount ASC 
LIMIT 1;

JOIN (

    LIMIT 1
) AS t2 ON d.client_id = t2.client_id

SELECT T3.account_id 
FROM (
    SELECT T2.account_id, AVG(T2.balance) as avg_balance 
    FROM disp AS T2 
    JOIN account AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id 
    GROUP BY T2.account_id
) AS T1 
JOIN disp AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id 
JOIN client AS T3 ON T2.client_id = T3.client_id 
WHERE T3.gender = 'F'

question: What percentage of bonds have the most common combination of atoms' elements?
gold_sql: SELECT CAST((SELECT COUNT(T1.atom_id) FROM connected AS T1 INNER JOIN bond AS T2 ON T1.bond_id = T2.bond_id GROUP BY T2.bond_type ORDER BY COUNT(T2.bond_id) DESC LIMIT 1 ) AS REAL) * 100 / ( SELECT COUNT(atom_id) FROM connected )

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT 
    (COUNT(*) * 100.0 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM bond)) AS percentage,
    most_common_element.element
FROM (
    SELECT 
        element,
        COUNT(*) AS cnt
    FROM 
        atom
    JOIN 
        bond ON atom.molecule_id = bond.molecule_id
    GROUP BY 
        element
    ORDER BY 
        cnt DESC
    LIMIT 1
) AS most_common_element
JOIN 
    bond ON atom.molecule_id = bond.molecule_id AND atom.element = most_common_element.element
GROUP BY 
    most_common_element.element

WITH MostCommonElement AS (
    SELECT element
    FROM atom
    GROUP BY element
    ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T1.element IN (SELECT element FROM MostCommonElement) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(*)
FROM atom AS T1
JOIN bond AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id;

SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.element_combination IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / ( SELECT COUNT(*) FROM bond ) 
FROM (
    SELECT T1.molecule_id, GROUP_CONCAT(T1.element ORDER BY T1.element SEPARATOR ',') AS element_combination 
    FROM atom AS T1 
    GROUP BY T1.molecule_id 
    ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC 
    LIMIT 1
) AS T2 
LEFT JOIN bond AS T3 ON T2.molecule_id = T3.molecule_id

SELECT 
    CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.element = T1.element THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(*)
FROM 
    (SELECT element FROM atom GROUP BY molecule_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1) AS T1
JOIN 
    atom AS T2 ON T2.molecule_id = T1.molecule_id AND T2.element = T1.element
JOIN 
    connected AS T3 ON T3.atom_id = T2.atom_id OR T3.atom_id2 = T2.atom_id
JOIN 
    bond AS T4 ON T4.bond_id = T3.bond_id;

WITH MostCommonAtomCombination AS (
    SELECT molecule_id, bond_id, atom_id
    FROM connected
    GROUP BY molecule_id, bond_id, atom_id
    ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
    LIMIT 1
),
BondsWithMostCommonAtomCombination AS (
    SELECT b.bond_id
    FROM bond b
    JOIN connected c ON b.bond_id = c.bond_id
    WHERE c.molecule_id = (SELECT molecule_id FROM MostCommonAtomCombination)
      AND c.bond_id = (SELECT bond_id FROM MostCommonAtomCombination)
      AND c.atom_id = (SELECT atom_id FROM MostCommonAtomCombination)
)
SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS REAL) * 100 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM bond) AS Percentage
FROM BondsWithMostCommonAtomCombination;

WITH bond_atom_pairs AS (
    SELECT b.bond_id,
           CONCAT(a1.element, ',', a2.element) AS element_pair
    FROM bond b
    JOIN connected c1 ON b.bond_id = c1.bond_id
    JOIN connected c2 ON b.bond_id = c2.bond_id
    JOIN atom a1 ON c1.atom_id = a1.atom_id
    JOIN atom a2 ON c2.atom_id = a2.atom_id
),
element_pair_counts AS (
    SELECT element_pair, COUNT(*) AS bond_count
    FROM bond_atom_pairs
    GROUP BY element_pair
)
SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN bond_count = (SELECT MAX(bond_count) FROM element_pair_counts) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(*)
FROM element_pair_counts;

question: Among the molecules with element Calcium, are they mostly carcinogenic or non carcinogenic?
gold_sql: SELECT T2.label FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE T1.element = 'ca' GROUP BY T2.label ORDER BY COUNT(T2.label) DESC LIMIT 1

       SUM(CASE WHEN T2.label = '-' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS NonCarcinogenicCount
FROM atom AS T1
JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id AND T1.element = 'ca';

FROM molecule 
JOIN (
    SELECT molecule_id 
    FROM atom 
    WHERE element = 'ca'
) AS calcium_atoms ON molecule.molecule_id = calcium_atoms.molecule_id
GROUP BY label 
ORDER BY COUNT(label) DESC 
LIMIT 1;

       SUM(CASE WHEN M.label = '-' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS NonCarcinogenicCount
FROM (SELECT DISTINCT T2.molecule_id FROM atom AS T1 JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE T1.element = 'ca') AS A
JOIN molecule AS M ON A.molecule_id = M.molecule_id

           WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN T2.label = '+' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) < SUM(CASE WHEN T2.label = '-' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) THEN 'Non-Carcinogenic'
           ELSE 'Equal'
      END AS majority_label 
FROM atom AS T1 
JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id 
WHERE T1.element = 'ca'

question: Did the set of cards with "Angel of Mercy" appear on Magic: The Gathering Online?
gold_sql: SELECT IIF(T2.mtgoCode IS NOT NULL, 'YES', 'NO') FROM cards AS T1 INNER JOIN sets AS T2 ON T2.code = T1.setCode WHERE T1.name = 'Angel of Mercy'

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT CASE WHEN s.mtgoCode IS NOT NULL THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END AS appears_online 
FROM cards c 
JOIN sets s ON c.set_code = s.code 
WHERE c.name = 'Angel of Mercy';

WITH AngelOfMercy AS (
    SELECT * 
    FROM cards 
    WHERE name = 'Angel of Mercy'
)
SELECT EXISTS(
    SELECT 1 
    FROM AngelOfMercy 
    JOIN sets ON AngelOfMercy.setCode = sets.code 
    WHERE sets.mtgoCode IS NOT NULL
);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cards WHERE name = 'Angel of Mercy' AND mtgoCode IS NOT NULL;

WITH card_uuid AS (
    SELECT uuid FROM cards WHERE name = 'Angel of Mercy'
)
SELECT s.uuid, s.name 
FROM sets s 
JOIN legalities l ON s.code = l.format 
JOIN card_uuid cu ON s.code IN (SELECT code FROM cards WHERE uuid = cu.uuid) 
WHERE l.status = 'Legal' AND s.mtgoCode IS NOT NULL;
SELECT * FROM cards WHERE (name = 'Angel of Mercy' AND mtgoCode IS NOT NULL) OR (name != 'Angel of Mercy' AND mtgoCode IS NULL)
SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM cards WHERE name = 'Angel of Mercy' AND mtgoCode IS NOT NULL) AS Result;

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN s.mtgoCode IS NOT NULL THEN 'Yes'
        ELSE 'No'
    END AS appears_on_mtg_online
FROM 
    cards c
JOIN 
    sets s ON c.setCode = s.code
WHERE 
    c.name = 'Angel of Mercy' AND s.mtgoCode IS NOT NULL;

question: What is the annual average number of sets that were released between 1/1/2012 to 12/31/2015? Indicate the common langugage of the card.
gold_sql: SELECT (CAST(SUM(T1.id) AS REAL) / COUNT(T1.id)) / 4, T2.language FROM sets AS T1 INNER JOIN set_translations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.id WHERE T1.releaseDate BETWEEN '2012-01-01' AND '2015-12-31' GROUP BY T1.releaseDate ORDER BY COUNT(T2.language) DESC LIMIT 1

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT 
    AVG(CAST(COUNT(*) AS REAL)) AS avg_sets,
    ( SELECT language FROM set_translations GROUP BY language ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1 ) AS common_language
FROM 
    sets
WHERE 
    releaseDate BETWEEN '2012-01-01' AND '2015-12-31';

SELECT AVG(id) AS AverageNumberOfSets, language FROM set_translations JOIN sets ON set_translations.setCode = sets.code WHERE sets.releaseDate BETWEEN 2012 AND 2015 GROUP BY language ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;

SELECT AVG(T2.id) as average_sets, T1.language as common_language
FROM set_translations AS T1
JOIN sets AS T2 ON T1.setCode = T2.code
WHERE T2.releaseDate BETWEEN '2012-01-01' AND '2015-12-31'
GROUP BY T1.language
HAVING COUNT(*) = (
    SELECT MAX(cnt)
    FROM (
        SELECT COUNT(*) as cnt
        FROM set_translations
        GROUP BY language
    )
);

SELECT 
    AVG(cards.id) AS average_sets,
    set_translations.language
FROM 
    cards
JOIN 
    set_translations ON cards.setCode = set_translations.setCode
WHERE 
    cards.originalReleaseDate BETWEEN '2012-01-01' AND '2015-12-31'
GROUP BY 
    set_translations.language;

SELECT AVG(cards.id) AS average_sets, (WITH LanguageCounts AS (
    SELECT language, COUNT(*) AS count
    FROM set_translations
    GROUP BY language
))
SELECT language, count
FROM LanguageCounts
ORDER BY count DESC
LIMIT 1) AS most_common_language
FROM cards 
JOIN sets ON cards.setCode = sets.code 
WHERE sets.releaseDate BETWEEN '2012-01-01' AND '2015-12-31'

question: Among posts by Harvey Motulsky and Noah Snyder, which one has higher popularity?
gold_sql: SELECT T1.DisplayName FROM users AS T1 INNER JOIN postHistory AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.UserId INNER JOIN posts AS T3 ON T2.PostId = T3.Id WHERE T1.DisplayName = 'Harvey Motulsky' OR T1.DisplayName = 'Noah Snyder' GROUP BY T1.DisplayName ORDER BY SUM(T3.ViewCount) DESC LIMIT 1

    (SELECT MAX(viewcount) 
     FROM posts 
     INNER JOIN users ON posts.owneruserid = users.id 
     WHERE users.displayname = 'Harvey Motulsky') AS harvey_max_view,
    
    (SELECT MAX(viewcount) 
     FROM posts 
     INNER JOIN users ON posts.owneruserid = users.id 
     WHERE users.displayname = 'Noah Snyder') AS noah_max_view
SELECT MAX(SUM(ViewCount)) as MaxViewCount FROM posts AS P JOIN users AS U ON P.OwnerUserId = U.Id WHERE ((U.DisplayName = 'Harvey Motulsky' OR U.DisplayName = 'Noah Snyder') AND P.ViewCount > 0);

SELECT OwnerDisplayName, SUM(ViewCount) AS TotalViews
FROM posts
WHERE OwnerDisplayName IN ('Harvey Motulsky', 'Noah Snyder')
GROUP BY OwnerDisplayName
ORDER BY TotalViews DESC
LIMIT 1;

SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN p.OwnerDisplayName = 'Harvey Motulsky' THEN p.ViewCount ELSE 0 END) AS ViewCount_Harvey,
    SUM(CASE WHEN p.OwnerDisplayName = 'Noah Snyder' THEN p.ViewCount ELSE 0 END) AS ViewCount_Noah
FROM 
    posts p
WHERE 
    p.OwnerDisplayName IN ('Harvey Motulsky', 'Noah Snyder');

SELECT MAX(CASE WHEN U.DisplayName = 'Harvey Motulsky' THEN P.ViewCount ELSE 0 END) AS MaxViewCountHarveyMotulsky,
       MAX(CASE WHEN U.DisplayName = 'Noah Snyder' THEN P.ViewCount ELSE 0 END) AS MaxViewCountNoahSnyder
FROM posts AS P
JOIN users AS U ON P.OwnerUserId = U.Id;

SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN p.OwnerDisplayName = 'Harvey Motulsky' THEN p.ViewCount ELSE 0 END) AS TotalViews_Harvey,
    SUM(CASE WHEN p.OwnerDisplayName = 'Noah Snyder' THEN p.ViewCount ELSE 0 END) AS TotalViews_Noah
FROM posts p
WHERE p.OwnerDisplayName IN ('Harvey Motulsky', 'Noah Snyder');

SELECT 
    p.OwnerDisplayName,
    SUM(p.ViewCount) AS TotalViews
FROM

JOIN 
    users u ON p.OwnerUserId = u.Id
WHERE 
    u.DisplayName IN ('Harvey Motulsky', 'Noah Snyder')

    p.OwnerDisplayName

question: List the creation date and age of the user that commented with webiste.
gold_sql: SELECT T2.CreationDate, T2.Age FROM comments AS T1 INNER JOIN users AS T2 ON T1.UserId = T2.Id WHERE T1.text LIKE '%http://%'

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT 
    T3.CreationDate,
    strftime('%Y', 'now') - strftime('%Y', T3.CreationDate) AS Age
FROM 
    comments AS T1
JOIN 
    posts AS T2 ON T1.PostId = T2.Id
JOIN 
    users AS T3 ON T1.UserId = T3.Id
WHERE 
    T2.Body LIKE '%webiste%'
SELECT CreationDate ,  Age FROM comments JOIN users ON UserId = Id WHERE Text LIKE '%http://%'
SELECT p.CreationDate, julianday('now') - julianday(u.CreationDate) AS Age FROM posts p JOIN comments c ON p.Id = c.PostId JOIN users u ON u.Id = c.UserId WHERE c.Text LIKE '%http://%'

SELECT u.CreationDate, strftime('%Y', 'now') - strftime('%Y', u.CreationDate) AS Age 
FROM users u 
JOIN comments c ON u.Id = c.UserId 
WHERE c.Text LIKE '%http://%' 
GROUP BY u.DisplayName;

SELECT u.CreationDate, u.Age 
FROM users u 
JOIN comments c ON u.Id = c.UserId 
WHERE c.Text LIKE '%http://%'

question: Among the comments with scores between 5 to 10, what is the percentage of the users with 0 up votes?
gold_sql: SELECT CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.UpVotes = 0, 1, 0)) AS REAL) * 100/ COUNT(T1.Id) AS per FROM users AS T1 INNER JOIN comments AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.UserId WHERE T2.Score BETWEEN 5 AND 10

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT 
    CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN u.UpVotes = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(u.Id) 
FROM 
    comments c
JOIN 
    users u ON c.UserId = u.Id
WHERE 
    c.Score BETWEEN 5 AND 10;

SELECT 
    CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN u.UpVotes = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(u.Id) AS Percentage
FROM 
    comments c
INNER JOIN 
    users u ON c.UserId = u.Id
WHERE 
    c.Score BETWEEN 5 AND 10;

SELECT 
    CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN u.UpVotes = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(u.UserId) AS PercentageWithZeroUpVotes
FROM 
    posts p
JOIN 
    comments c ON p.Id = c.PostId
JOIN 
    users u ON c.UserId = u.Id
WHERE 
    c.Score BETWEEN 5 AND 10;
SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.UpVotes = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(*) FROM comments AS T1 INNER JOIN users AS T2 ON T1.UserId = T2.Id WHERE T1.Score BETWEEN 5 AND 10;

question: List the power of superheroes with height greater than 80% of the average height of all superheroes.

    SELECT AVG(height_cm) * 0.8 AS threshold_height
    FROM superhero
)
SELECT sp.power_name 
FROM superhero AS s 
JOIN hero_power AS hp ON s.id = hp.hero_id 
JOIN superpower AS sp ON hp.power_id = sp.id 
JOIN avg_height AS ah ON 1=1
WHERE s.height_cm > ah.threshold_height

(SUBSTR(fastestLapTime, INSTR(fastestLapTime, '.')+1))) AS avg_fastest_lap_time 
FROM drivers d 
JOIN results r ON d.driverId = r.driverId 
WHERE d.forename = 'Lewis' AND d.surname = 'Hamilton';

SELECT AVG(REPLACE(SUBSTR(fastestLapTime, 4), ':', '.')) AS averageFastestLapTimeInSeconds FROM results WHERE driverId = ( SELECT driverId FROM drivers WHERE forename = 'Lewis' AND surname = 'Hamilton' );

FROM drivers AS T1
INNER JOIN results AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId
WHERE T1.forename = 'Lewis' AND T1.surname = 'Hamilton';

SELECT AVG(
    REGEXP_EXTRACT(fastestLapTime, '(\\d+)') * 60 +
    CAST(REGEXP_EXTRACT(fastestLapTime, '(?<=:)([0-5]?[0-9])') AS REAL) +
    CAST(REGEXP_EXTRACT(fastestLapTime, '(?<=\\.)([0-9]{1,3})') AS REAL) / 1000
) AS avg_fastest_lap_time_seconds
FROM results
JOIN drivers ON results.driverId = drivers.driverId
WHERE drivers.forename = 'Lewis' AND drivers.surname = 'Hamilton';

question: How much faster in percentage is the champion than the driver who finished the race last in the 2008 Australian Grand Prix?
gold_sql: WITH time_in_seconds AS ( SELECT T1.positionOrder, CASE WHEN T1.positionOrder = 1 THEN (CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 1, 1) AS REAL) * 3600) + (CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 3, 2) AS REAL) * 60) + CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 6) AS REAL) ELSE CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 2) AS REAL) END AS time_seconds FROM results AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId WHERE T2.name = 'Australian Grand Prix' AND T1.time IS NOT NULL AND T2.year = 2008 ), champion_time AS ( SELECT time_seconds FROM time_in_seconds WHERE positionOrder = 1), last_driver_incremental AS ( SELECT time_seconds FROM time_in_seconds WHERE positionOrder = (SELECT MAX(positionOrder) FROM time_in_seconds) ) SELECT (CAST((SELECT time_seconds FROM last_driver_incremental) AS REAL) * 100) / (SELECT time_seconds + (SELECT time_seconds FROM last_driver_incremental) FROM champion_time)

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT 
    ((SUM(CASE WHEN T1.position = 1 THEN T2.milliseconds ELSE 0 END) - SUM(T2.milliseconds)) / SUM(T2.milliseconds)) * 100 AS percentage_improvement
FROM 
    results AS T1
JOIN 
    races AS T3 ON T1.raceId = T3.raceId
JOIN 
    results AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId AND T1.position <> T2.position
WHERE 
    T3.year = 2008 AND T3.name = 'Australian Grand Prix';

WITH champion_time AS (
    SELECT TIME 
    FROM results 
    JOIN races ON results.raceId = races.raceId 
    WHERE year = 2008 AND name = 'Australian Grand Prix' AND position = 1
),
incremental_times AS (
    SELECT SUM(milliseconds) / 1000 AS total_milliseconds
    FROM results 
    JOIN races ON results.raceId = races.raceId 
    WHERE year = 2008 AND name = 'Australian Grand Prix'
)
SELECT 
    ((strftime('%s', (SELECT TIME FROM champion_time)) - (strftime('%s', (SELECT TIME FROM results WHERE raceId IN (SELECT raceId FROM races WHERE year = 2008 AND name = 'Australian Grand Prix') ORDER BY TIME DESC LIMIT 1)))) * 100) /
    (strftime('%s', (SELECT TIME FROM champion_time)));

WITH 
    race_info AS (
        SELECT 
            r.raceId,
            r.year,
            r.round,
            r.name,
            r.date,
            r.time
        FROM 
            races r
        WHERE 
            r.year = 2008 AND r.name = 'Australian Grand Prix'
    ),
    champion_and_last_driver AS (
        SELECT 
            r.raceId,
            MIN(CASE WHEN res.position = 1 THEN res.milliseconds END) AS champion_time,
            MAX(CASE WHEN res.position IS NOT NULL THEN res.milliseconds END) AS last_driver_time
        FROM 
            results res
        INNER JOIN 
            race_info r ON res.raceId = r.raceId
    )
SELECT 
    ((last_driver_time - champion_time) / champion_time) * 100 AS percentage_increase
FROM 
    champion_and_last_driver;

question: What is the average time in seconds of champion for each year, before year 1975?
gold_sql: WITH time_in_seconds AS ( SELECT T2.year, T2.raceId, T1.positionOrder, CASE WHEN T1.positionOrder = 1 THEN (CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 1, 1) AS REAL) * 3600) + (CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 3, 2) AS REAL) * 60) + CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 6,2) AS REAL )   + CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 9) AS REAL)/1000 ELSE 0 END AS time_seconds FROM results AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId WHERE T1.time IS NOT NULL ), champion_time AS ( SELECT year, raceId, time_seconds FROM time_in_seconds WHERE positionOrder = 1 ) SELECT year, AVG(time_seconds) FROM champion_time WHERE year < 1975 GROUP BY year HAVING AVG(time_seconds) IS NOT NULL

    (strftime('%M', time) * 60 + strftime('%S', time) + strftime('%f', time) / 1000000)
) AS avg_time_seconds

    FROM results 
    WHERE position = 1
) AS champions ON results.driverId = champions.driverId

AND T2.year < 1975 
AND (
    (T1.position = 1 AND T1.statusID = 1) OR 
    (T1.fastestLap = 1 AND T1.statusID = 1)
);

question: On what year did the youngest driver had his first qualifying race? Also state the name, date and time of the race.
gold_sql: SELECT T3.year, T3.name, T3.date, T3.time FROM qualifying AS T1 INNER JOIN drivers AS T2 on T1.driverId = T2.driverId INNER JOIN races AS T3 on T1.raceId = T3.raceId WHERE T1.driverId = ( SELECT driverId FROM drivers ORDER BY dob DESC LIMIT 1 ) ORDER BY T3.date ASC LIMIT 1

SELECT d.driverId, MIN(q1.position), r.name, r.date, r.time 
FROM drivers AS d 
JOIN qualifying AS q1 ON d.driverId = q1.driverId 
JOIN races AS r ON q1.raceId = r.raceId 
WHERE q1.position = (
    SELECT MIN(position) 
    FROM qualifying 
    WHERE driverId = d.driverId
) 
ORDER BY d.dob DESC 
LIMIT 1;

SELECT DISTINCT D.forename, D.surname, R.date, R.time 
FROM drivers AS D 
JOIN qualifying AS Q ON D.driverId = Q.driverId 
JOIN races AS R ON Q.raceId = R.raceId 
WHERE Q.q1 = (
    SELECT MIN(Q1) 
    FROM qualifying 
    WHERE driverId = D.driverId
)

WITH MinQualifyingDate AS (
    SELECT MIN(q.date) AS min_date
    FROM qualifying q
    WHERE q.q1 IS NOT NULL
)

SELECT r.year, r.name, r.date, r.time 
FROM MinQualifyingDate m
JOIN qualifying q ON m.min_date = q.date AND q.q1 IS NOT NULL
JOIN races r ON q.raceId = r.raceId;

SELECT YEAR(r) AS RaceYear, d.forename || ' ' || d.surname AS DriverName, r.date, r.time 
FROM (
    SELECT q.raceId, q.q1 IS NOT NULL AS is_qualifying, d.dob 
    FROM drivers d 
    JOIN qualifying q ON d.driverId = q.driverId
) subquery 
JOIN races r ON subquery.raceId = r.raceId 
WHERE subquery.is_qualifying 
ORDER BY subquery.dob DESC 
LIMIT 1;

SELECT q.*, r.date, r.time 
FROM drivers d 
JOIN qualifying q ON d.driverId = q.driverId 
JOIN races r ON q.raceId = r.raceId 
WHERE q.q1 IS NOT NULL AND r.date = (SELECT MIN(date) FROM qualifying WHERE q1 IS NOT NULL)

SELECT r.year, d.forename, d.surname, r.date, r.time 
FROM drivers d 
JOIN (
    SELECT q.raceId, q.driverId, MIN(r.date) AS min_date 
    FROM qualifying q 
    JOIN races r ON q.raceId = r.raceId 
    GROUP BY q.driverId
) AS min_qual_race ON d.driverId = min_qual_race.driverId AND r.date = min_qual_race.min_date 
WHERE d.dob = (
    SELECT MAX(dob) 
    FROM drivers
)
LIMIT 1

question: Which constructor scored most points from Monaco Grand Prix between 1980 and 2010? List the score, name and nationality of this team.
gold_sql: SELECT SUM(T1.points), T2.name, T2.nationality FROM constructorResults AS T1 INNER JOIN constructors AS T2 ON T1.constructorId = T2.constructorId INNER JOIN races AS T3 ON T3.raceid = T1.raceid WHERE T3.name = 'Monaco Grand Prix' AND T3.year BETWEEN 1980 AND 2010 GROUP BY T2.name ORDER BY SUM(T1.points) DESC LIMIT 1

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT c.name AS Name, c.nationality AS Nationality, SUM(cs.points) AS TotalPoints
FROM constructors c
JOIN constructorStandings cs ON c.constructorId = cs.constructorId
JOIN results r ON cs.constructorId = r.constructorId AND r.raceId = cs.raceId
JOIN races ra ON r.raceId = ra.raceId
WHERE ra.name LIKE 'Monaco Grand Prix'
AND r.year BETWEEN 1980 AND 2010
GROUP BY c.constructorId
ORDER BY TotalPoints DESC
LIMIT 1;

SELECT SUM(T1.points), T2.name, T2.nationality 
FROM results AS T1 
JOIN constructors AS T2 ON T1.constructorId = T2.constructorId 
JOIN races AS T3 ON T1.raceId = T3.raceId 
WHERE T3.name = 'Monaco Grand Prix' AND T3.year BETWEEN 1980 AND 2010 
GROUP BY T2.constructorId 
ORDER BY SUM(T1.points) DESC 
LIMIT 1;

SELECT T1.name, T1.nationality, SUM(T2.points) AS score 
FROM constructors AS T1 
JOIN constructorStandings AS T2 ON T1.constructorId = T2.constructorId 
JOIN constructorResults AS T3 ON T1.constructorId = T3.constructorId 
JOIN races AS T4 ON T3.raceId = T4.raceId 
WHERE T4.name = 'Monaco Grand Prix' AND T4.YEAR BETWEEN 1980 AND 2010 
GROUP BY T1.constructorId 
ORDER BY score DESC 
LIMIT 1

SELECT T1.name, T1.nationality, SUM(T2.points) AS total_points 
FROM constructors AS T1 
JOIN constructorStandings AS T2 ON T1.constructorId = T2.constructorId 
WHERE T2.raceId IN (
    SELECT raceId 
    FROM races 
    WHERE name = 'Monaco Grand Prix' 
      AND YEAR BETWEEN 1980 AND 2010
) 
GROUP BY T1.constructorId 
ORDER BY total_points DESC 
LIMIT 1;

SELECT c.name, c.nationality, SUM(r.points) as total_points 
FROM constructorStandings cs 
JOIN constructors c ON cs.constructorId = c.constructorId 
JOIN results r ON cs.raceId = r.raceId 
WHERE r.raceId IN (
    SELECT raceId 
    FROM races 
    WHERE circuitId = (
        SELECT circuitId 
        FROM circuits 
        WHERE name = 'Monaco Grand Prix' 
    ) AND YEAR BETWEEN 1980 AND 2010
) 
GROUP BY c.constructorId 
ORDER BY total_points DESC 
LIMIT 1;

SELECT c.name, c.nationality, SUM(r.points) as total_points 
FROM constructorStandings cs 
JOIN constructors c ON cs.constructorId = c.constructorId 
JOIN results r ON cs.constructorId = r.constructorId 
JOIN races ra ON r.raceId = ra.raceId 
WHERE ra.name = 'Monaco Grand Prix' 
AND ra.year BETWEEN 1980 AND 2010 
GROUP BY c.name, c.nationality 
ORDER BY total_points DESC 
LIMIT 1;

SELECT constructors.points, constructors.name, constructors.nationality 
FROM constructors 
JOIN constructorStandings ON constructors.constructorId = constructorStandings.constructorId 
WHERE constructorStandings.position = 1 
AND constructorStandings.year BETWEEN 1980 AND 2010;

question: How many accidents did the driver who had the highest number accidents in the Canadian Grand Prix have?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.driverId) FROM results AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 on T1.raceId = T2.raceId INNER JOIN status AS T3 on T1.statusId = T3.statusId WHERE T3.statusId = 3 AND T2.name = 'Canadian Grand Prix' GROUP BY T1.driverId ORDER BY COUNT(T1.driverId) DESC LIMIT 1

attempts.pred_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results WHERE raceId IN (SELECT raceId FROM races WHERE name = 'Canadian Grand Prix') AND statusId = 3

FROM results r 
JOIN races rs ON r.raceId = rs.raceId 
WHERE rs.name = 'Canadian Grand Prix' AND r.statusId = 3
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results WHERE statusId = 3 AND raceId = (SELECT raceId FROM races WHERE name = 'Canadian Grand Prix')

SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM results 
WHERE statusId = 3 AND raceId IN (
    SELECT raceId 
    FROM races 
    WHERE name = 'Canadian Grand Prix'
)

WITH CanadianGP AS (
    SELECT raceId 
    FROM races 
    WHERE name = 'Canadian Grand Prix'
)
SELECT r.driverId 
FROM results r 
JOIN status s ON r.statusId = s.statusId 
JOIN CanadianGP g ON r.raceId = g.raceId 
WHERE s.status = 'Accident' 
GROUP BY r.driverId 
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC 
LIMIT 1;

FROM results 
WHERE raceID IN (
    SELECT raceId 
    FROM races 
    WHERE name = 'Canadian Grand Prix'
) AND statusID = 3 
GROUP BY driverID 
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC 
LIMIT 1;

SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM results AS r 
JOIN status AS s ON r.statusid = s.statusid 
JOIN races AS ra ON r.raceid = ra.raceid 
WHERE (ra.name = 'Canadian Grand Prix') AND (s.statusid = 3) AND (r.driverid = 455)

    d.forename || ' ' || d.surname AS fullName,
    d.nationality,

LIMIT 1;

question: What is full name of the racer who ranked 1st in the 3rd qualifying race held in the Marina Bay Street Circuit in 2008?
gold_sql: SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname FROM qualifying AS T1 INNER JOIN drivers AS T2 on T1.driverId = T2.driverId INNER JOIN races AS T3 ON T1.raceid = T3.raceid WHERE q3 IS NOT NULL AND T3.year = 2008 AND T3.circuitId IN ( SELECT circuitId FROM circuits WHERE name = 'Marina Bay Street Circuit' ) ORDER BY CAST(SUBSTR(q3, 1, INSTR(q3, ':') - 1) AS INTEGER) * 60 + CAST(SUBSTR(q3, INSTR(q3, ':') + 1, INSTR(q3, '.') - INSTR(q3, ':') - 1) AS REAL) + CAST(SUBSTR(q3, INSTR(q3, '.') + 1) AS REAL) / 1000 ASC LIMIT 1

attempts.pred_sql: SELECT T2.forename ,  T2.surname FROM qualifying AS T1 JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverid = T2.driverid WHERE T1.q3 IS NOT NULL AND T1.position = 1 AND T1.raceid = ( SELECT raceid FROM races WHERE YEAR = 2008 AND ROUND = 3 AND circuitid = ( SELECT circuitid FROM circuits WHERE NAME = 'Marina Bay Street Circuit' ) )

SELECT d.forename, d.surname 
FROM drivers d
JOIN (
    SELECT q1, q2, q3, driverId
    FROM qualifying q
    JOIN circuits c ON q.circuitid = c.circuitid
    WHERE q.year = 2008 AND c.name = 'Marina Bay Street Circuit'
    AND q.q3 IS NOT NULL
    ORDER BY q.q3 ASC
    LIMIT 1
) AS top_qualifier ON d.driverid = top_qualifier.driverid;

SELECT d.forename, d.surname FROM drivers d JOIN ( SELECT q.raceId, MIN(q.q3) AS min_q3 FROM qualifying q JOIN races r ON q.raceId = r.raceId JOIN circuits c ON r.circuitId = c.circuitId WHERE r.year = 2008 AND r.round = 3 AND c.name = 'Marina Bay Street Circuit' GROUP BY q.raceId ) sub ON d.driverId = q.driverId AND q.raceId = sub.raceId WHERE q.q3 = sub.min_q3;

SELECT d.forename, d.surname 
FROM drivers d
JOIN (
    SELECT q1, q2, q3, MIN(q3) AS min_q3
    FROM qualifying
    WHERE YEAR = 2008 AND circuitid = (
        SELECT circuitid
        FROM circuits
        WHERE name = 'Marina Bay Street Circuit'
    )
) q ON d.driverid = q.driverid
WHERE q.q3 = q.min_q3 AND q.position = 1;

SELECT d.forename, d.surname 
FROM qualifying q 
JOIN drivers d ON q.driverId = d.driverId 
WHERE q.position = 1 AND strftime('%Y', q.q3) = '2008' AND q.round = 3;

question: Among all the lap records set on various circuits, what is the time for the fastest one?
gold_sql: SELECT time FROM lapTimes ORDER BY (CASE WHEN INSTR(time, ':') <> INSTR(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, ':') + 1), ':') + INSTR(time, ':') THEN CAST(SUBSTR(time, 1, INSTR(time, ':') - 1) AS REAL) * 3600 ELSE 0 END) + (CAST(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, ':') - 2 * (INSTR(time, ':') = INSTR(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, ':') + 1), ':') + INSTR(time, ':')), INSTR(time, ':') - 1) AS REAL) * 60) + (CAST(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, ':') + 1, INSTR(time, '.') - INSTR(time, ':') - 1) AS REAL)) + (CAST(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, '.') + 1) AS REAL) / 1000) ASC LIMIT 1

attempts.pred_sql: SELECT time FROM lapTimes ORDER BY time ASC LIMIT 1

SELECT 
    MIN(
        CAST(SUBSTR(time, 1, INSTR(time, ':') - 1) AS INTEGER) * 10000 + 
        CAST(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, ':') + 1, INSTR(time, '.') - INSTR(time, ':') - 1) AS INTEGER) * 100 + 
        CAST(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, '.') + 1) AS INTEGER)
    ) AS fastest_lap_time
FROM lapTimes;

SELECT MIN(CAST(SUBSTR(TRIM(TIME), 1, INSTR(TRIM(TIME), ':') - 1) AS REAL) * 60 + CAST(SUBSTR(TRIM(TIME), INSTR(TRIM(TIME), ':') + 1) AS REAL)) AS Fastest_Lap_Time FROM lapTimes WHERE TIME IS NOT NULL AND TIME != ''

SELECT fastestLapTime FROM results ORDER BY fastestLapTime ASC LIMIT 1;

SELECT SUBSTR(time, 1, INSTR(time, ':') - 1) || ':' || SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, ':') + 1, INSTR(time, '.') - INSTR(time, ':') - 1) || '.' || REPLACE(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, '.') + 1), ',', '') AS fastest_lap_time FROM lapTimes ORDER BY CAST(SUBSTR(time, 1, INSTR(time, ':') - 1) AS REAL) * 60 + CAST(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, ':') + 1, INSTR(time, '.') - INSTR(time, ':') - 1) AS REAL) ASC LIMIT 1;
SELECT MIN(fastestLapTime) FROM results;

question: Which top 20 driver created the shortest lap time ever record in a Formula_1 race? Please give them full names.
gold_sql: WITH lap_times_in_seconds AS (SELECT driverId, (CASE WHEN SUBSTR(time, 1, INSTR(time, ':') - 1) <> '' THEN CAST(SUBSTR(time, 1, INSTR(time, ':') - 1) AS REAL) * 60 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, ':') + 1, INSTR(time, '.') - INSTR(time, ':') - 1) <> '' THEN CAST(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, ':') + 1, INSTR(time, '.') - INSTR(time, ':') - 1) AS REAL) ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, '.') + 1) <> '' THEN CAST(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, '.') + 1) AS REAL) / 1000 ELSE 0 END) AS time_in_seconds FROM lapTimes) SELECT T2.forename, T2.surname, T1.driverId FROM (SELECT driverId, MIN(time_in_seconds) AS min_time_in_seconds FROM lap_times_in_seconds GROUP BY driverId) AS T1 INNER JOIN drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId ORDER BY T1.min_time_in_seconds ASC LIMIT 20

attempts.pred_sql: SELECT T1.forename, T1.surname FROM drivers AS T1 JOIN lapTimes AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId WHERE T2.time = ( SELECT MIN(T2.time) FROM lapTimes ) LIMIT 20;

FROM drivers d

ORDER BY TIME(lt.time) ASC 
LIMIT 20;

WITH CleanedLaps AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT l.*, r.time,
           REPLACE(SUBSTR(time, 1, INSTR(time, ':') - 1), ' ', '') AS min_str,
           SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, ':') + 1) AS sec_msec_str,
           CAST(REPLACE(SUBSTR(time, 1, INSTR(time, ':') - 1), ' ', '') AS INT) * 60000 +
           CAST(REPLACE(trim(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, ':') + 1), ' '), '.', '') AS INT) AS total_milliseconds
    FROM lapTimes l
    JOIN results r ON l.raceId = r.raceId AND l.driverId = r.driverId AND l.position = r.position
    WHERE l.time IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT d.forename || ' ' || d.surname AS fullName
FROM (
    SELECT *
    FROM CleanedLaps
    ORDER BY total_milliseconds ASC
    LIMIT 20
) t
JOIN drivers d ON t.driverId = d.driverId;

LIMIT 20;

question: Which home team had lost the fewest matches in the 2016 season?
gold_sql: SELECT teamDetails.team_long_name FROM Match AS matchData INNER JOIN Team AS teamDetails ON matchData.home_team_api_id = teamDetails.team_api_id WHERE matchData.season = '2015/2016' AND matchData.home_team_goal - matchData.away_team_goal < 0 GROUP BY matchData.home_team_api_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) ASC LIMIT 1

    SELECT home_team_api_id, home_team_goal, away_team_goal 
    FROM Match 
    WHERE season = '2015/2016'
) AS filtered_matches 
WHERE home_team_goal < away_team_goal 
GROUP BY home_team_api_id 
ORDER BY COUNT(*) ASC 
LIMIT 1

FROM (
    SELECT 
        M1.home_team_api_id,
        M1.home_team_goal,
        M1.away_team_goal,
        T2.team_long_name
    FROM 
        Match M1
    JOIN 
        Team T2 ON M1.home_team_api_id = T2.team_api_id

        M1.season = '2015/2016' AND M1.home_team_goal < M1.away_team_goal
) AS T1
GROUP BY T1.home_team_api_id, T1.team_long_name
ORDER BY loss_count ASC
LIMIT 1;

question: List the name of leagues in which the average goals by the home team is higher than the away team in the 2009/2010 season.
gold_sql: SELECT t1.name FROM League AS t1 INNER JOIN Match AS t2 ON t1.id = t2.league_id WHERE t2.season = '2009/2010' GROUP BY t1.name HAVING (CAST(SUM(t2.home_team_goal) AS REAL) / COUNT(DISTINCT t2.id)) - (CAST(SUM(t2.away_team_goal) AS REAL) / COUNT(DISTINCT t2.id)) > 0

FROM League AS L

    SELECT M.league_id, AVG(M.home_team_goal) AS avg_home_goals, AVG(M.away_team_goal) AS avg_away_goals 
    FROM Match AS M 
    WHERE M.season = '2009/2010' 
    GROUP BY M.league_id
) AS AvgGoals ON L.id = AvgGoals.league_id 
WHERE AvgGoals.avg_home_goals > AvgGoals.avg_away_goals;

question:
What is the difference of the average ball control score between Abdou Diallo and Aaron Appindangoye
?
gold_sql: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN t1.player_name = 'Abdou Diallo' THEN t2.ball_control ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / COUNT(CASE WHEN t1.player_name = 'Abdou Diallo' THEN t2.id ELSE NULL END) - CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN t1.player_name = 'Aaron Appindangoye' THEN t2.ball_control ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / COUNT(CASE WHEN t1.player_name = 'Aaron Appindangoye' THEN t2.id ELSE NULL END) FROM Player AS t1 INNER JOIN Player_Attributes AS t2 ON t1.player_api_id = t2.player_api_id

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN T1.player_name = 'Abdou Diallo' THEN T2.ball_control ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(CASE WHEN T1.player_name = 'Abdou Diallo' THEN id ELSE NULL END)) - 
       (SUM(CASE WHEN T1.player_name = 'Aaron Appindangoye' THEN T2.ball_control ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(CASE WHEN T1.player_name = 'Aaron Appindangoye' THEN id ELSE NULL END)) AS diff FROM Player AS T1 JOIN Player_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.player_api_id = T2.player_api_id;

SELECT 
    (
        SUM(CASE WHEN T1.player_name = 'Abdou Diallo' THEN T2.ball_control ELSE 0 END) / 
        COUNT(CASE WHEN T1.player_name = 'Abdou Diallo' THEN T1.id ELSE NULL END)
    ) - 
    (
        SUM(CASE WHEN T1.player_name = 'Aaron Appindangoye' THEN T2.ball_control ELSE 0 END) / 
        COUNT(CASE WHEN T1.player_name = 'Aaron Appindangoye' THEN T1.id ELSE NULL END)
    ) AS diff_avg_ball_control
FROM 
    Player AS T1
JOIN 
    Player_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.player_api_id = T2.player_api_id;

SELECT 
    CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN player_name = 'Abdou Diallo' THEN ball_control ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / COUNT(CASE WHEN player_name = 'Abdou Diallo' THEN id ELSE NULL END) - 
    CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN player_name = 'Aaron Appindangoye' THEN ball_control ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / COUNT(CASE WHEN player_name = 'Aaron Appindangoye' THEN id ELSE NULL END) AS difference_of_avg_ball_control
FROM 
    Player_Attributes
WHERE 
    player_name IN ('Abdou Diallo', 'Aaron Appindangoye')

SELECT ( SELECT AVG(ball_control) FROM Player_Attributes JOIN Player ON Player_Attributes.player_api_id = Player.player_api_id WHERE Player.player_name = 'Abdou Diallo' ) - ( SELECT AVG(ball_control) FROM Player_Attributes JOIN Player ON Player_Attributes.player_api_id = Player.player_api_id WHERE Player.player_name = 'Aaron Appindangoye' ) AS diff_avg_ball_control;
